user_message: Give me the manager view on this Woodgrove Labs email thread. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-01-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:05:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:09:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:14:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:27:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:30:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Technical Deep-Dive Transcript -> Architecture + Blockers template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
